# csv

```go
import "github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go/internal/io/csv"
```

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This package implements the io.FileReader and io.FileWriter interfaces for CSV format.

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This file contains utilities for auto\-detecting CSV format properties such as delimiter characters and header rows.

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This package implements the io.FileReader and io.FileWriter interfaces for CSV format.

The CSV reader supports:

- Auto\-detection of delimiters \(comma, tab, semicolon, pipe\)
- Header row detection and parsing
- Compressed file reading \(gzip, zstd, etc.\)
- Chunked reading for memory\-efficient processing
- NULL value handling via configurable null string
- Comment line skipping
- Automatic type inference for columns

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This file implements type inference for CSV columns.

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go.

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This file contains utility types and functions for the CSV package.

Package csv provides CSV file reading and writing capabilities for dukdb\-go. This file implements the CSV writer for exporting DataChunks to CSV format.

## Constants

<a name="parseIntBase"></a>Constants for integer parsing.

```go
const (
    // Base 10 for integer parsing.
    parseIntBase = 10
    // Bit size for 64-bit integers.
    parseIntBitSize = 64
    // Bit size for 64-bit floats.
    parseFloatBitSize = 64
)
```

<a name="DefaultSampleSize"></a>Default values for reader options.

```go
const (
    // DefaultSampleSize is the default number of rows to sample for type inference.
    DefaultSampleSize = 1000
    // DefaultMaxRowsPerChunk is the default maximum number of rows per DataChunk.
    DefaultMaxRowsPerChunk = storage.StandardVectorSize
    // DefaultQuote is the default quote character.
    DefaultQuote = '"'
    // DefaultDelimiter is the default field delimiter.
    DefaultDelimiter = ','
)
```

<a name="headerNumericThreshold"></a>headerNumericThreshold is the ratio of numeric values in data rows required to consider a non\-numeric first row as a header. If more than 50% of data values in a column are numeric but the first row value is not numeric, that column likely has a header.

```go
const headerNumericThreshold = 0.5
```

<a name="maxPeekSize"></a>maxPeekSize is the maximum number of bytes to peek for delimiter detection. 64KB provides enough sample data for reliable detection.

```go
const maxPeekSize = 64 * 1024
```

<a name="minLinesForConsistency"></a>minLinesForConsistency is the minimum number of lines needed to check delimiter consistency across multiple rows.

```go
const minLinesForConsistency = 2
```

## Variables

<a name="_"></a>Verify Reader implements FileReader interface.

```go
var _ fileio.FileReader = (*Reader)(nil)
```

<a name="_"></a>Verify Writer implements FileWriter interface.

```go
var _ fileio.FileWriter = (*Writer)(nil)
```

<a name="commonDelimiters"></a>commonDelimiters lists the delimiters to try during auto\-detection. These are tried in order: comma, tab, semicolon, and pipe.

```go
var commonDelimiters = []rune{',', '\t', ';', '|'}
```

<a name="dateFormats"></a>dateFormats lists supported date formats in order of preference.

```go
var dateFormats = []string{
    "2006-01-02",
    "01/02/2006",
    "02-Jan-2006",
    "2006/01/02",
    "02/01/2006",
    "Jan 02, 2006",
    "January 2, 2006",
}
```

<a name="timestampFormats"></a>timestampFormats lists supported timestamp formats in order of preference.

```go
var timestampFormats = []string{
    time.RFC3339,
    time.RFC3339Nano,
    "2006-01-02T15:04:05",
    "2006-01-02 15:04:05",
    "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z",
    "2006-01-02 15:04:05.000000",
    "2006-01-02 15:04:05.000",
    "01/02/2006 15:04:05",
    "02/01/2006 15:04:05",
}
```

<a name="ConvertValue"></a>
## func [ConvertValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L308>)

```go
func ConvertValue(s string, targetType dukdb.Type) (any, bool)
```

ConvertValue converts a string value to the appropriate Go type based on the target DuckDB type. This is used when filling typed vectors. Returns the converted value and whether conversion succeeded. For NULL values \(empty string\), returns nil and true.

<a name="DetectHeader"></a>
## func [DetectHeader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L147>)

```go
func DetectHeader(lines [][]string) bool
```

DetectHeader attempts to detect if the first row is a header by comparing types. This is a heuristic: if the first row contains different "types" than subsequent rows, it is likely a header.

<a name="TryParseBoolean"></a>
## func [TryParseBoolean](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L212>)

```go
func TryParseBoolean(s string) (value, ok bool)
```

TryParseBoolean attempts to parse a string as a boolean. Accepts: true/false, yes/no, 1/0, t/f, y/n \(case insensitive\). Returns the parsed value and whether parsing succeeded.

<a name="analyzeDelimiters"></a>
## func [analyzeDelimiters](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L59>)

```go
func analyzeDelimiters(lines []string) (map[rune]int, map[rune][]int)
```

analyzeDelimiters counts delimiter occurrences and tracks consistency across lines. Returns two maps: total scores \(sum of occurrences\) and consistency data \(per\-line counts for each delimiter\).

<a name="countNumericValues"></a>
## func [countNumericValues](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L174>)

```go
func countNumericValues(rows [][]string, colIdx int) int
```

countNumericValues counts how many rows have a numeric value in the given column. This is used for header detection \- if data rows are mostly numeric but the first row is not, the first row is likely a header.

<a name="detectDelimiter"></a>
## func [detectDelimiter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L36>)

```go
func detectDelimiter(r *bufio.Reader, sampleSize int) (rune, error)
```

detectDelimiter tries to auto\-detect the delimiter from sample lines. It peeks at the beginning of the file and analyzes which common delimiter appears most consistently across lines. Consistency is preferred over raw count because a delimiter should appear the same number of times on each line \(representing column separators\).


formatInterval formats an Interval as a string.

```go
func generateColumnNames(count int) []string
```

<a name="isConsistent"></a>
## func [isConsistent](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L129>)

```go
func isConsistent(counts []int) bool
```

isConsistent checks if all values in the slice are equal. For delimiter detection, this means the delimiter appears the same number of times on each line, which is expected for proper CSV format.

<a name="looksNumeric"></a>
## func [looksNumeric](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L189>)

```go
func looksNumeric(str string) bool
```

looksNumeric returns true if the string appears to be a numeric value. It checks for integer and decimal number patterns, allowing for thousands separators \(comma or space\) and optional sign prefix.

<a name="pickBestDelimiter"></a>
## func [pickBestDelimiter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/detect.go#L90>)

```go
func pickBestDelimiter(scores map[rune]int, consistency map[rune][]int) (rune, error)
```

pickBestDelimiter selects the best delimiter based on scores and consistency. Priority is given to delimiters that appear consistently \(same count on each line\) since that indicates proper column separation. Falls back to highest raw count if no consistent delimiter is found.


<a name="Reader"></a>
## type [Reader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/reader.go#L20-L39>)

Reader implements the FileReader interface for CSV files. It reads CSV data into DataChunks with type inference. The reader supports lazy initialization \- schema detection and type inference happen on first read or schema access.

```go
type Reader struct {
    // reader is the underlying Go CSV reader.
    reader *csv.Reader
    // closer handles cleanup of file handles and decompressors.
    closer io.Closer
    // opts contains user-specified and detected options.
    opts *ReaderOptions
    // columns holds column names (from header or generated).
    columns []string
    // columnTypes holds the inferred type for each column.
    columnTypes []dukdb.Type
    // initialized tracks whether schema detection has been performed.
    initialized bool
    // eof indicates end of file has been reached.
    eof bool
    // bufferedRows holds rows read during detection but not yet returned.
    bufferedRows [][]string
    // inferrer handles type inference for columns.
    inferrer *TypeInferrer
}
```

<a name="NewReader"></a>
### func [NewReader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/reader.go#L45>)

```go
func NewReader(r io.Reader, opts *ReaderOptions) (*Reader, error)
```

NewReader creates a new CSV reader from an io.Reader. If opts is nil, default options are used. The delimiter is auto\-detected if not specified in the options. Note that the io.Reader is consumed and should not be used after calling this function.

<a name="NewReaderFromPath"></a>

```go
func NewReaderFromPath(path string, opts *ReaderOptions) (*Reader, error)
```

NewReaderFromPath creates a new CSV reader from a file path. Handles compression detection and decompression automatically.

<a name="Reader.Schema"></a>

```go
func (r *Reader) Schema() ([]string, error)
```

Schema returns the column names after the reader has been initialized. This method triggers lazy initialization if not already done, which may involve reading the first row to detect column names from the header.

<a name="Reader.Types"></a>

```go
func (r *Reader) Types() ([]dukdb.Type, error)
```

Types returns the inferred column types after the reader has been initialized. This method triggers lazy initialization if not already done.

<a name="Reader.addRowToChunk"></a>
### func \(\*Reader\) [addRowToChunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/reader.go#L276>)

```go
func (r *Reader) addRowToChunk(chunk *storage.DataChunk, record []string, rowIdx int) error
```

addRowToChunk adds a CSV row to the DataChunk at the specified index. It maps each field in the record to its corresponding column vector, converting string values to the appropriate typed values based on the inferred column types. Missing fields are treated as NULL, and values matching NullStr are NULL.

```go
func (r *Reader) ensureInitialized() error
```

ensureInitialized performs lazy initialization \(schema detection\).

```go
func (r *Reader) initializeSchema() error
```

initializeSchema reads the first row and sets up column names and types. It samples rows for type inference, then buffers those rows for later reading.

<a name="ReaderOptions"></a>
## type [ReaderOptions](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/options.go#L21-L56>)

ReaderOptions contains configuration options for the CSV reader.

```go
type ReaderOptions struct {
    // Delimiter is the field separator character.
    // If zero, auto-detection is attempted.
    Delimiter rune

    // Quote is the character used to quote fields containing special characters.
    // Default is double quote (").
    Quote rune

    // Header indicates whether the first row contains column names.
    // If false and not auto-detected, columns are named "column0", "column1", etc.
    Header bool

    // NullStr is the string representation of NULL values.
    // Empty string matches empty fields.
    NullStr string

    // Skip is the number of rows to skip before reading data (or header).
    Skip int

    // Comment is the character that marks comment lines to skip.
    // Lines starting with this character are ignored.
    // A zero value means no comment character is set.
    Comment rune

    // SampleSize is the number of rows to sample for delimiter and header detection.
    // Default is DefaultSampleSize (1000).
    SampleSize int

    // MaxRowsPerChunk limits the number of rows returned per DataChunk.
    // Default is DefaultMaxRowsPerChunk (2048).
    MaxRowsPerChunk int

    // IgnoreErrors indicates whether to skip malformed rows instead of failing.
    IgnoreErrors bool
}
```

<a name="DefaultReaderOptions"></a>
### func [DefaultReaderOptions](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/options.go#L59>)

```go
func DefaultReaderOptions() *ReaderOptions
```

DefaultReaderOptions returns ReaderOptions with sensible defaults.

<a name="ReaderOptions.applyDefaults"></a>
### func \(\*ReaderOptions\) [applyDefaults](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/options.go#L74>)

```go
func (o *ReaderOptions) applyDefaults()
```

applyDefaults fills in zero values with defaults.

<a name="TypeInferrer"></a>
## type [TypeInferrer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L27-L32>)

TypeInferrer performs type inference on CSV column data. It samples values from each column and determines the most specific type that can represent all sampled values.

```go
type TypeInferrer struct {
    // sampleSize is the maximum number of rows to sample for type inference.
    sampleSize int
    // columnTypes holds the inferred type per column.
    columnTypes []dukdb.Type
}
```

<a name="NewTypeInferrer"></a>
### func [NewTypeInferrer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L36>)

```go
func NewTypeInferrer(size int) *TypeInferrer
```

NewTypeInferrer creates a new TypeInferrer with the given sample size. If sampleSize is \<= 0, DefaultSampleSize \(1000\) is used.

<a name="TypeInferrer.InferTypes"></a>
### func \(\*TypeInferrer\) [InferTypes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L61>)

```go
func (ti *TypeInferrer) InferTypes(samples [][]string) []dukdb.Type
```

InferTypes analyzes sample rows and returns the inferred types for each column. The inference strategy is to find the most specific type that works for ALL non\-empty values in each column. Type priority \(most to least specific\):

1. BOOLEAN \(true/false, yes/no, 1/0\)
2. INTEGER \(int64 within int32 range\)
3. BIGINT \(int64 for larger integers\)
4. DOUBLE \(float64\)
5. DATE \(YYYY\-MM\-DD or common formats\)
6. TIMESTAMP \(ISO 8601 or common formats\)
7. VARCHAR \(fallback\)

Empty strings and the configured null string are treated as NULL and do not affect type inference \(a column with only NULLs becomes VARCHAR\).

<a name="Writer"></a>
## type [Writer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L67-L84>)

Writer implements the FileWriter interface for CSV files. It writes DataChunks to CSV format with configurable options.

```go
type Writer struct {
    // writer is the underlying Go CSV writer.
    writer *csv.Writer
    // rawWriter is the underlying io.Writer (for compression wrappers).
    rawWriter io.Writer
    // closer handles cleanup of file handles and compressors.
    closer io.Closer
    // opts contains user-specified options.
    opts *WriterOptions
    // columns holds column names (set via SetSchema).
    columns []string
    // columnTypes holds the type for each column (derived from first chunk).
    columnTypes []dukdb.Type
    // forceQuoteSet is a set of column names that should always be quoted.
    forceQuoteSet map[string]bool
    // headerWritten tracks whether the header row has been written.
    headerWritten bool
}
```

<a name="NewWriter"></a>
### func [NewWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L88>)

```go
func NewWriter(w io.Writer, opts *WriterOptions) (*Writer, error)
```

NewWriter creates a new CSV writer to an io.Writer. If opts is nil, default options are used.

<a name="NewWriterToPath"></a>
### func [NewWriterToPath](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L101>)

```go
func NewWriterToPath(path string, opts *WriterOptions) (*Writer, error)
```

NewWriterToPath creates a new CSV writer to a file path. Handles compression based on options or file extension.

<a name="createWriter"></a>
### func [createWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L141>)

```go
func createWriter(w io.Writer, closer io.Closer, opts *WriterOptions) (*Writer, error)
```

createWriter creates a Writer with the given configuration.

<a name="Writer.Close"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L402>)

```go
func (w *Writer) Close() error
```

Close flushes any buffered data and releases resources.

<a name="Writer.SetSchema"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [SetSchema](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L165>)

```go
func (w *Writer) SetSchema(columns []string) error
```

SetSchema sets the column names for the output file. Must be called before WriteChunk if Header option is enabled.

<a name="Writer.WriteChunk"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [WriteChunk](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L175>)

```go
func (w *Writer) WriteChunk(chunk *storage.DataChunk) error
```

WriteChunk writes a DataChunk to the CSV file. On the first call, column types are inferred from the chunk. If Header is enabled and a schema was set, the header row is written first.

<a name="Writer.forceQuoteValue"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [forceQuoteValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L387>)

```go
func (w *Writer) forceQuoteValue(s string) string
```

forceQuoteValue ensures a value is quoted in the CSV output. The csv.Writer handles quoting automatically based on content, but we need to force quotes for specified columns. We do this by ensuring the value contains a character that triggers quoting.

<a name="Writer.formatValue"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [formatValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L270>)

```go
func (w *Writer) formatValue(value any, typ dukdb.Type) string
```

formatValue converts a value to its CSV string representation.

<a name="Writer.writeHeader"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [writeHeader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L208>)

```go
func (w *Writer) writeHeader() error
```

writeHeader writes the header row to the CSV file.

<a name="Writer.writeRow"></a>
### func \(\*Writer\) [writeRow](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L219>)

```go
func (w *Writer) writeRow(chunk *storage.DataChunk, rowIdx int) error
```

writeRow writes a single row from the DataChunk.

<a name="WriterOptions"></a>
## type [WriterOptions](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L19-L40>)

WriterOptions contains configuration options for the CSV writer.

```go
type WriterOptions struct {
    // Delimiter is the field separator character (default: comma).
    Delimiter rune

    // Quote is the character used to quote fields containing special characters.
    // Default is double quote (").
    Quote rune

    // Header indicates whether to write a header row with column names.
    // Default is true.
    Header bool

    // NullStr is the string representation of NULL values.
    // Default is empty string.
    NullStr string

    // ForceQuote is a list of column names that should always be quoted.
    ForceQuote []string

    // Compression specifies the compression type for file output.
    Compression fileio.Compression
}
```

<a name="DefaultWriterOptions"></a>
### func [DefaultWriterOptions](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L43>)

```go
func DefaultWriterOptions() *WriterOptions
```

DefaultWriterOptions returns WriterOptions with sensible defaults.

<a name="WriterOptions.applyDefaults"></a>
### func \(\*WriterOptions\) [applyDefaults](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L55>)

```go
func (o *WriterOptions) applyDefaults()
```

applyDefaults fills in zero values with defaults.

<a name="combinedCloser"></a>
## type [combinedCloser](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/utils.go#L12-L15>)

combinedCloser closes both a decompressor and underlying file. This ensures proper cleanup when reading compressed files.

```go
type combinedCloser struct {
    decompressor io.Closer
    file         io.Closer
}
```

<a name="combinedCloser.Close"></a>
### func \(\*combinedCloser\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/utils.go#L19>)

```go
func (c *combinedCloser) Close() error
```

Close closes both the decompressor and the underlying file. The first error encountered is returned.

<a name="combinedWriteCloser"></a>
## type [combinedWriteCloser](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L423-L426>)

combinedWriteCloser closes both a compressor and underlying file.

```go
type combinedWriteCloser struct {
    compressor io.WriteCloser
    file       io.Closer
}
```

<a name="combinedWriteCloser.Close"></a>
### func \(\*combinedWriteCloser\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/writer.go#L430>)

```go
func (c *combinedWriteCloser) Close() error
```

Close closes both the compressor and the underlying file. The compressor must be closed first to flush compressed data.

<a name="typeCandidate"></a>
## type [typeCandidate](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L119-L127>)

typeCandidate tracks which types are still viable for a column.

```go
type typeCandidate struct {
    canBoolean   bool
    canInteger   bool
    canBigint    bool
    canDouble    bool
    canDate      bool
    canTimestamp bool
    hasValues    bool // whether any non-null values were seen
}
```

<a name="typeCandidate.resolveType"></a>
### func \(\*typeCandidate\) [resolveType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/io/csv/infer.go#L180>)

```go
func (tc *typeCandidate) resolveType() dukdb.Type
```

resolveType determines the final type based on candidate flags. Priority order: BOOLEAN \> INTEGER \> BIGINT \> DOUBLE \> DATE \> TIMESTAMP \> VARCHAR
